title: Retrieval-Grounded Agent Systems for Automated Related-Work Drafting

content.abstract: This paper studies retrieval-grounded agent systems for automated related-work drafting and claims improvements in citation faithfulness and topical coverage.
content.introduction: The paper motivates the need for grounded related-work generation in academic writing workflows.
content.method: The proposed system decomposes the task into retrieval, clustering, evidence selection, draft planning, and citation-constrained generation.
content.experiments: The paper evaluates on a benchmark of writing tasks and compares against direct prompting, RAG, and a planner-generator baseline. It reports citation precision, expert preference, and topic coverage.
content.conclusion: The authors conclude that agentic decomposition improves reliability and usefulness.

claims: The proposed retrieval-grounded agent system improves citation faithfulness.
Task decomposition improves topical coverage and expert preference.
The approach is practical for academic writing assistance.
known_constraints: No rebuttal or supplementary implementation details available
target_output: full reviewer report with weak accept or weak reject recommendation
